news.title: Lucknow development body extends deadline for Atal Nagar housing scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority has extended the registration deadline for its Atal Nagar Housing Scheme to November 21, offering 2,496 1-BHK and 2-BHK flats for low and middle-income groups. Meanwhile, the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel Housing Scheme in Dalibagh, featuring 72 flats on land recovered from illegal possession, saw an overwhelming response and concluded its registration on November 3.

news.title: Noida airport nearly ready but survey finds 94% business plots along Yamuna expressway unused
news.para: A government survey reveals a significant portion of industrial land along the Yamuna Expressway remains undeveloped, with 94% of plots vacant just months before the Noida International Airport's launch. This contrasts sharply with Noida's robust industrial occupancy, highlighting stalled growth potential in the YEIDA region despite rising demand.

news.title: Gujarat plans five new satellite towns to ease urban expansion
news.para: Gujarat's government is transforming five towns—Kalol, Sanand, Savli, Bardoli, and Hirasar—into fully developed satellite towns around its major cities. A high-level committee will oversee infrastructure development to ensure synchronized growth, aiming to ease pressure on urban centers and foster balanced development and investment.

news.title: Mumbai: Court rejects anticipatory bail plea of builder after death at construction site
news.para: A Mumbai court denied a builder anticipatory bail after a young woman died when a concrete brick fell from his under-construction building. Photographs revealed torn safety nets and a lack of pedestrian protection, violating safety guidelines. The judge noted the builder concealed a prior bail application, indicating he did not approach the court with clean hands.

news.title: Consumer court orders Nagpur builder to refund homebuyer's money with interest
news.para: A consumer commission has ordered a real estate firm to refund Rs3 lakh with 9% interest to a doctor couple, along with Rs30,000 for harassment and Rs10,000 for costs. The firm was found deficient in service for retaining the booking amount after cancellation, deeming it unjust enrichment without a forfeiture clause.

news.title: Aquilius raises $1.1 billion for Asia's largest real estate secondaries fund
news.para: Aquilius Investment Partners has achieved a remarkable milestone by raising $1.1 billion for their second Asia Pacific real estate secondaries fund. This is now recognized as the largest fund of its type in the region, strategically aimed at acquiring existing stakes in private equity funds.

news.title: Arvind SmartSpaces' net profit dips 57.61% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Arvind SmartSpaces reported a 57.61% decline in net profit for Q2 FY26, with profit after tax at ₹18.06 crore. Total income also dipped by 46.51% to ₹144.03 crore. Despite this, the company signed a new joint development project and maintained a negative net debt of ₹(32) crore.

news.title: Ambuja Cements' profit surges over four-fold to ₹2,302 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Ambuja Cements' profit after tax surged over four times in Q2 FY26compared to the previous year. This growth was driven by strong revenue increases in both cement and ready-mix concrete businesses. Ambuja Cements also achieved its highest ever sales volume for the second quarter.

news.title: Motorola leases over 200 seats with Smartworks in Kolkata
news.para: Motorola Solutions has secured over 200 seats with a 60-month lease at Smartworks' Kolkata center. This expansion contributes to Smartworks' significant growth, having leased over one million sq. ft. across four cities in the last quarter. The managed office operator now boasts a portfolio of 10.08 million sq. ft. of leased space.

news.title: Khata clarity sought for properties in dispute in Bengaluru
news.para: ​The original landowner is said to have repeatedly appealed to the BBMP not to issue any form of Khata, citing a temporary court injunction on the land. Citizens demand scrutiny of records, transparent process to verify authenticity before granting regularisation to properties, especially those under litigation.

news.title: Smartworks leases over 8.15 lakh sq ft workspace at Eastbridge, Mumbai
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces has secured a significant lease of over 8.15 lakh sq ft at Regalia Office Parks' Eastbridge development in Vikhroli West, Mumbai. This new center is slated to open in Q4 2026. This expansion follows a recent 5.57 lakh sq ft lease at Tata Realty's Intellion Park in Navi Mumbai.

news.title: Shriram Properties to jointly develop five-acre land in South Bengaluru
news.para: Shriram Properties is set to develop a premium villa community on five acres in Bengaluru's Bannerghatta Road. This prime location offers excellent connectivity and amenities, making it attractive to homebuyers. The project, with an estimated gross development value exceeding ₹350 crore, is slated for launch in fiscal year 2026, promising exclusivity and convenience.

news.title: Land lease defaulters owe Kerala government ₹665 crore
news.para: The state has identified 584 individuals and organizations occupying government-leased land without paying dues or violating lease terms, accumulating arrears of Rs 665.30 crore. Despite significant financial dues, only a negligible amount of land has been reclaimed, and the government lacks a compiled record of total illegally occupied land.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: 100 more flats handed over in third phase at Shantipalli Awas Yojana
news.para: Bhubaneswar Municipal Corporation and Bhubaneswar Development Authority have allotted over 100 more units at Shantipalli Awas Yojana, bringing the total beneficiaries to 560. This marks the third phase of allotment for Keshari Residency, one of three affordable housing projects under PM Awas Yojana in the city. The project, a public-private partnership, will eventually comprise 1,300 dwelling units.

news.title: HC directs Haryana government to explain delay in constituting SEIAA after February
news.para: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has ordered the State of Haryana to provide a specific affidavit. This affidavit must clarify why the State Environment Impact Assessment Authority has not been constituted after February 2025. The court also seeks all correspondence between the state and the Union government on this matter.

news.title: Real estate companies from other cities flock to NCR on rising demand, prices
news.para: In a surprising turn of events, developers from Mumbai and Bengaluru are setting their sights on the National Capital Region, where property prices are outpacing growth seen elsewhere. Real estate giants are not just launching new projects; they are also significantly enlarging their market footprint.

news.title: Register Lucknow development body's properties at special camp from Nov 3-15
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is organizing a 13-day 'single-window' camp from November 3-15 to expedite pending property registrations.  LDA officials will prepare documents for the first 10 days, with the registry department finalizing registrations on-site during the last three days. Allottees who have paid in full or 80% or more are being contacted to complete their property registries.

news.title: PNB Housing Finance shortlists four for MD & CEO position
news.para: PNB Housing Finance is close to appointing a new Managing Director and CEO. Four candidates have been shortlisted, with Ajai Shukla of Tata Capital Housing Finance leading the race. The company has submitted the names to regulators for approval. This appointment follows multiple leadership changes in recent years. An update on the selection process is expected soon.
